steps.title: Write both surface areas
steps.content:
Let $s$ be the side length of each square base. One prism has height $80$.
$$K=2s^2+4s(80)=2s^2+320s$$
After gluing the prisms along a square base, the resulting prism has height $160$.
$$2s^2+4s(160)=2s^2+640s$$

steps.title: Use the given ratio
steps.content:
Set the combined surface area equal to $\frac{33}{17}K$.
$$\frac{2s^2+640s}{2s^2+320s}=\frac{33}{17}$$
Cancel the common factor $2s$.
$$\frac{s+320}{s+160}=\frac{33}{17}$$
